<!--
Transcluded standing caveat for [CAUSAL] results. Stated once, reused everywhere —
edit HERE only. Included via {{< include ../_caveats-causal.qmd >}} (from a chapter in
chapters/) or {{< include _caveats-causal.qmd >}} (from a top-level file).
-->

::: {.callout-important title="Causal status — [CAUSAL]"}
The estimate(s) here are identified by **randomisation**. Under the locked DAG
(@sec-dag) the intervention group is a randomised root, so the intention-to-treat effect
is the one quantity the design licenses as **causal**. The own baseline and linear age
are **precision** terms — they sharpen the estimate but are *not* what licenses the
causal reading — and intervention sessions / dose are **never** conditioned on (a DAG
collider). A **positive** effect means the intervention raised the outcome.
:::
